fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2e0570b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification delivery
+ * adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture: The NotificationService dispatches notifications to adapters
+ * based on channel. Each adapter handles delivery for exactly one channel
+ * (email, webhook, SMS, etc.).
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel to NotificationChannel type in src/models/notification.ts
+ * 2. Create an adapter class implementing this interface
+ * 3. Register it with NotificationService.registerAdapter()
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - Adapters must be stateless with respect to delivery (no internal queue)
+ * - Adapters must return a NotificationResult for every send() call
+ * - Adapters must not throw — errors go in NotificationResult.error
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
+   * Must not throw — return a NotificationResult with success: false on error.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

  * TaskFlow Pro — A task and order management system.
  *
  * Architecture:
- * - Service Layer: UserService, OrderService (business logic)
+ * - Service Layer: UserService, OrderService, NotificationService (business logic)
  * - Repository Layer: UserRepository, OrderRepository (data access via Repository pattern)
+ * - Adapter Layer: EmailAdapter, etc. (notification delivery via NotificationAdapter interface)
  * - Utility Layer: Database, Logger, Pagination (infrastructure)
  * - Event System: EventBus with typed events (decoupled communication)
  *
  * Key architectural decisions:
  * 1. Repository pattern for all data access (no direct DB calls from services)
- * 2. Event-driven notifications (services emit events, listeners handle side effects)
+ * 2. Event-driven notifications exclusively via EventBus (no direct service calls)
+ * 3. All notification delivery through NotificationAdapter implementations
  */
 
 // Models
@@ -49,6 +51,20 @@ export { UserService } from './services/user.service.js';
 export { OrderService } from './services/order.service.js';
 export { NotificationService } from './services/notification.service.js';
 
+// Notification types
+export type {
+  Notification,
+  NotificationChannel,
+  NotificationType,
+  NotificationPayload,
+  NotificationPriority,
+  NotificationResult,
+} from './models/notification.js';
+
+// Adapters
+export type { NotificationAdapter } from './adapters/notification.adapter.js';
+export { EmailAdapter } from './adapters/email.adapter.js';
+
 // Config
 export { createConfig, defaultConfig } from './config/app.config.js';
 export type { AppConfig } from './config/app.config.js';

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..7d4c0c0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model — core types for the notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: All notifications flow through EventBus. Services emit
+ * domain events (e.g., order:created), the NotificationService listens
+ * and dispatches to registered NotificationAdapters (email, webhook, etc.).
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
+ * - All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()
+ * - No direct service-to-service calls for notification delivery
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification channels. Each channel has a corresponding
+ * adapter that implements NotificationAdapter.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'webhook' | 'in-app' | 'sms';
+
+/**
+ * Notification types correspond to the domain events that trigger them.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order-confirmation'
+  | 'order-status-update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'user-updated';
+
+/**
+ * Priority levels for notification delivery ordering.
+ */
+export type NotificationPriority = 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'critical';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID */
+  id: string;
+  /** The type of notification being sent */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** The channel this notification should be delivered through */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, webhook URL, etc.) */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject/title */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content */
+  body: string;
+  /** Delivery priority */
+  priority: NotificationPriority;
+  /** When the notification was created */
+  createdAt: Date;
+  /** Optional key-value metadata for adapter-specific data */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result of a notification delivery attempt.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** The notification that was sent */
+  notificationId: string;
+  /** The channel used for delivery */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed */
+  error?: string;
+  /** When delivery was attempted */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * A persisted notification record combining payload and delivery results.
+ */
+export interface Notification {
+  /** The full notification payload */
+  payload: NotificationPayload;
+  /** Results from each delivery attempt */
+  results: NotificationResult[];
+  /** Current delivery status */
+  status: 'pending' | 'sent' | 'failed' | 'partial';
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-3QuZlf.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter

cons: Already flagged as inconsistent in codebase comments
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, synchronous-only by default. The codebase comments already identify mixed patterns as a maintenance problem.
This is the current state and the codebase explicitly identifies it as a problem. Adding more code on top of an inconsistent foundation would make things worse for 11 other developers.

constraints: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit()
All delivery adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
No direct service-to-service calls for notifications
11 other developers will build on this foundation
parameters.context: The codebase has both EventBus and CallbackRegistry patterns. The existing notification service mixes both, which the code comments explicitly flag as inconsistent. Need to pick ONE pattern for the notification system foundation that 11 other developers will build on.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the better fit: (1) already the primary pattern in the codebase, (2) supports async handlers natively, (3) provides event logging for debugging/testing, (4) enables loose coupling so new notification channels can be added without modifying existing services, (5) typed event discriminated unions provide compile-time safety. The CallbackRegistry usage for order:status-changed will be migrated to EventBus for consistency.
parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus exclusively — no direct calls or CallbackRegistry

parameters.content:
/**
 * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification delivery
 * adapters must implement.
 *
 * Architecture: The NotificationService dispatches notifications to adapters
 * based on channel. Each adapter handles delivery for exactly one channel
 * (email, webhook, SMS, etc.).
 *
 * To add a new notification channel:
 * 1. Add the channel to NotificationChannel type in src/models/notification.ts
 * 2. Create an adapter class implementing this interface
 * 3. Register it with NotificationService.registerAdapter()
 *
 * Constraints:
 * - Adapters must be stateless with respect to delivery (no internal queue)
 * - Adapters must return a NotificationResult for every send() call
 * - Adapters must not throw — errors go in NotificationResult.error
 */

import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';

export interface NotificationAdapter {
  /** The channel this adapter handles */
  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;

  /**
   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
   * Must not throw — return a NotificationResult with success: false on error.
   */
  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
}

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted
 * through EventBus. The NotificationService and other listeners
 * subscribe independently — no direct coupling.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * TaskFlow Pro — A task and order management system.
 *
 * Architecture:
 * - Service Layer: UserService, OrderService, NotificationService (business logic)
 * - Repository Layer: UserRepository, OrderRepository (data access via Repository pattern)
 * - Adapter Layer: EmailAdapter, etc. (notification delivery via NotificationAdapter interface)
 * - Utility Layer: Database, Logger, Pagination (infrastructure)
 * - Event System: EventBus with typed events (decoupled communication)
 *
 * Key architectural decisions:
 * 1. Repository pattern for all data access (no direct DB calls from services)
 * 2. Event-driven notifications exclusively via EventBus (no direct service calls)
 * 3. All notification delivery through NotificationAdapter implementations
 */

parameters.detail:
Completed notification system foundation:

## Architecture Decision
Chose event-driven architecture exclusively via EventBus. Rejected CallbackRegistry (direct calls) and hybrid approaches. The codebase already had both patterns mixed, which was explicitly flagged as problematic.

## What was built
1. **src/models/notification.ts** — Core types: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationPriority, NotificationResult, Notification
2. **src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts** — NotificationAdapter interface (the contract all delivery adapters must implement)
3. **src/adapters/email.adapter.ts** — EmailAdapter implementing NotificationAdapter (stub delivery, real interface)
4. **src/services/notification.service.ts** — Refactored to be purely event-driven. Subscribes to EventBus events, dispatches through registered adapters. Has registerAdapter() for adding channels.
5. **src/services/order.service.ts** — Migrated order:status-changed from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
6. **src/index.ts** — Updated exports for all new types and adapters
7. **tests/services/notification.service.test.ts** — Updated tests for new architecture (10 tests)
8. **tests/services/order.service.test.ts** — Fixed test expectation for unified EventBus pattern

## Constraints for other developers
- All notifications MUST flow through EventBus.emit()
- All delivery adapters MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
- No direct service-to-service calls for notifications
- To add a new event trigger: define event in event-types.ts, add handler in NotificationService
- To add a new channel: implement NotificationAdapter, register with registerAdapter()

## Status
- TypeScript compiles cleanly (npx tsc --noEmit)
- All 73 tests pass (7 test files)
parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture via EventBus, NotificationAdapter interface, EmailAdapter, refactored NotificationService

parameters.detail: The notification system uses event-driven architecture exclusively. Services emit domain events via EventBus, and NotificationService subscribes and dispatches to NotificationAdapter implementations. The CallbackRegistry still exists in the codebase (src/utils/callback-registry.ts) but is no longer used for notifications. Do not re-introduce direct callback patterns for notification delivery.
parameters.summary: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls or CallbackRegistry for notifications

parameters.detail: To add a new notification channel: (1) add channel to NotificationChannel type in src/models/notification.ts, (2) create adapter implementing NotificationAdapter interface, (3) register with NotificationService.registerAdapter(). Adapters must not throw — errors go in NotificationResult.error.
parameters.summary: All notification delivery adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface from src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: implement event-driven notification system foundation

Unify notification architecture on EventBus exclusively, removing the
mixed CallbackRegistry pattern. Define NotificationAdapter interface
for pluggable delivery channels, with EmailAdapter as first implementation.

- Add core notification types (NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, etc.)
- Add NotificationAdapter interface in src/adapters/
- Add EmailAdapter stub with real interface
- Refactor NotificationService to dispatch through registered adapters
- Migrate order:status-changed from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- Update all tests for unified event-driven pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
